qa_matrix.question_text: Please provide full details of condition including symptoms, treatment and complications if any.
qa_matrix.captured_response: It was like at one point, it was a hole in my body. I had pain, yes, pain. So I showed it to the doctor. He said that a separate passage forms from the toilet's tube and it opens at a different point. So, that's what the operation was for. For that, I was at home for about three months, two months, because it required proper rest and for the whole wound to heal, it took two months. No, nothing. It was properly done.

qa_matrix.captured_response: No, there's nothing like that, I'll tell you.

qa_matrix.question_text: Other than what you have already told us, have you had a medical condition that has required any of the following within the last 5 years a)Any abnormal medical findings or pending test results, scheduled or planned or prescribed medical tests such as imaging scan reports (e.g. x-ray, ultrasound, MRI, PET scan, CT scan etc.), biopsy, electrocardiogram (ECG), treadmill, Mammography, Endoscopy/Colonoscopy, PAP Smear, blood or urine test
qa_matrix.captured_response: No, that's what I did, I told you about it, right? Yes, that. Yes, that's it, nothing else was done.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Yes, a blood test under a government scheme showed thyroid.

qa_matrix.question_text: Other than what you have already told us, have you had a medical condition that has required any of the following within the last 5 years b) Medication or treatment that lasted more than 2 weeks or had been referred to a specialist doctor or hospital for any treatment/consultation and/or investigations (eg: Blood, ECG, MRI, Xray etc)
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Yes, took thyroid medicine for two months.

qa_matrix.question_text: Other than what you have already told us, have you had a medical condition that has required any of the following within the last 5 years c) Inability to perform your daily activities or absent from work more than two 2 consecutive weeks at a time
qa_matrix.captured_response: I had a fistula operation, which is called 'bhagandar' in Hindi. For that, I was at home for about three months, two months, because it required proper rest and for the whole wound to heal, it took two months.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Doctor asked for 'anytime' instead of 'last 5 years'. The event (fistula operation) was outside the 5-year timeframe.

qa_matrix.question_text: Have you suffered from any congenital defect or disorder or hereditary disease such as cerebral palsy, Down's syndrome, poliomyelitis
Have you or your spouse or partner been told to undergo tests or treatment, or received any medical advice or counselling in connection with sexually transmitted diseases, including HIV and AIDS?

Apart from what you have disclosed, are you experiencing any unexplained weight loss/gain or any sign/symptoms that you are likely to seek medical advice or treatment for or awaiting any surgical procedure.
qa_matrix.captured_response: No, ma'am, nothing like that, nothing.

Sir/Mam' ar e you in agr eement with the declaration that I have r ead out to you.

critical_issues: Customer's name in the MER ('Sakhare') does not match the name confirmed in the transcript ('Sathe').
Several mandatory questions from the MER were not asked by the doctor (e.g., respiratory, liver, blood, neurological, gastrointestinal, musculoskeletal, eye disorders).
The doctor failed to ask specific follow-up questions regarding the customer's hypothyroidism (e.g., if caused by cancer/tumor, or if it led to heart/memory problems).
The MER incorrectly states 'No' for doctor recommending treatment for high blood pressure, despite the customer stating 'medicines were given' at the time it was high.
The MER incorrectly states 'No' for abnormal medical findings/tests within the last 5 years, despite the customer disclosing a blood test that showed thyroid issues.
The MER incorrectly states 'No' for medication/treatment lasting more than 2 weeks within the last 5 years, despite the customer disclosing taking thyroid medicine for two months.
The MER incorrectly states the last follow-up for fistula as 2009, while the customer stated 2010.

The doctor deviated from the MER's timeframe for the 'inability to perform daily activities' question, asking for 'anytime' instead of 'last 5 years', which led to the disclosure of a condition (fistula) outside the specified period.
Customer-side prompting/coaching was detected during the call, indicating potential external influence on the customer's responses.
The customer exhibited significant hesitation and evasion when asked for details about their thyroid condition and when attempting to end the call.
recommendations: The doctor must ensure strict adherence to the MER questionnaire, asking all listed questions and their follow-ups.
The doctor needs to accurately capture all disclosed medical information, especially regarding medication, test results, and dates, ensuring consistency between the conversation and the MER.
The doctor should clarify any discrepancies in customer's name and other personal particulars immediately during the call.
Training should be provided to the doctor on how to handle customer hesitation and potential third-party prompting professionally, while still obtaining all necessary information.
The doctor should strictly adhere to the specified timeframes (e.g., 'last 5 years') for questions as per the MER.

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer expressed frustration: 'So ma'am, it's not relevant for you to call me and ask me to tell this or that. How will I tell you now?'
Customer expressed frustration: 'You should have informed me earlier whether I would receive a call or not.'
Customer expressed frustration: 'That's why I am not from your doctor's profession, I don't have good English either.'
Customer attempted to end the call multiple times due to another call or perceived irrelevance of questions.

validation_errors: Customer name 'Prashant Digambar Sathe' in transcript vs 'Prashant Digambar Sakhare' in MER.
Customer stated 'medicines were given' for BP, but MER says 'No' for doctor recommending treatment.
Customer took thyroid medicine for 2 months, but MER says 'No' for medication/treatment > 2 weeks.
Customer stated last follow-up for fistula in 2010, MER says 2009.
Customer disclosed a blood test with abnormal finding (thyroid), but MER says 'No' for abnormal medical findings/tests.
Doctor asked about inability to perform daily activities for 'anytime' instead of 'last 5 years' as per MER.

examples: customer: There's a lot. doctor: Ma'am, one moment. customer: No, one moment. customer: What is your name? customer: Please give me the phone for a minute, ma'am.
customer: So ma'am, it's not relevant for you to call me and ask me to tell this or that. How will I tell you now?
customer: You should have informed me earlier whether I would receive a call or not.
customer: That's why I am not from your doctor's profession, I don't have good English either.
customer: One second, ma'am, my... my call is on waiting.
customer: Ma'am, one minute, I'm getting another call. One minute, one minute.
